name: Kritical Agentic Mega Pipeline
author: Joshua Finley
copyright: (c) 2026 Kritical Pty Ltd. All rights reserved.
purpose: Coordinate stock coding agents, Kilo-style agent surfaces, SCXCode MCP/tools, free-router lanes, SCX-native lanes, and Kritical supervisor lanes without modifying upstream tools.

router.name: Kritical Dual Plane
router.supervisor_command: python mux/Invoke-KritAgentSupervisor.py --mode dry-run --prompt "<design prompt>" --sqlite out/supervisor.sqlite --report out/supervisor.md

kilo.notes: Use Kilo as the high-autonomy IDE/CLI agent when installed. Keep its native gateway/account flow intact; Kritical snippets only add local free-router and SCX planes.

cline.notes: Best open-source VS Code tool for explicit MCP-heavy plan/act workflows.

roo-code.notes: Roo Code follows the same OpenAI-compatible/MCP overlay pattern as Cline where installed.

continue.notes: Best for policy-as-code config and lower-autonomy chat/context workflows.

codex.notes: Use SCX shim for Responses/tool-shape normalization; free-router mode is optional for OpenAI-compatible chat paths.

claude-code.notes: Native Claude Code remains untouched; overlay only applies where custom endpoint support is available.

opencode.notes: Terminal-native peer to Claude Code/Codex when installed.

hermes.notes: Hermes Agent is useful for persistent-memory autonomous workflows. Configure through its native provider settings; Kritical supplies free-router/OpenRouter and MCP references.

openclaw.notes: Use only with tight repo boundaries, least-privilege tool permissions, and sandboxed worktrees. Kritical does not auto-enable broad OS/browser/email control.

kiloclaw.notes: Hosted OpenClaw through Kilo. Treat model spend as account-managed; Kritical can supply instructions and MCP shape but does not bypass Kilo billing or governance.

aider.notes: Useful for patch-focused repo work with explicit git diffs.

github-copilot-agent-hq.notes: Use for GitHub issue/PR-native delegation when available. Kritical does not override account-managed model routing.

amazon-q-developer.notes: Use for AWS/IAM/CDK/CloudFormation-heavy tasks. Kritical does not inject free-router credentials into account-managed AWS auth.

gemini-code-assist.notes: Use for GCP/Android-heavy tasks. Free Gemini API keys remain available through the free-router plane where direct OpenAI-compatible routing is needed.

cursor.notes: Cursor is not a VS Code extension. Treat it as a separate app; do not mutate stock VS Code config to support it.

windsurf.notes: Windsurf is a separate editor. Treat it as optional external surface, not a Kritical-required dependency.

supervisor.batching: Group cheap/free exploration first, reserve SCX lanes for synthesis, validation, hard debugging, and final response.
supervisor.response_policy: Return a concise operator-ready plan, changed-file summary, verification evidence, and explicit unresolved blockers.

kill_switch: Delete the generated pack directory, stop local routers/shims, or ignore these snippets. Underlying Kilo, Claude Code, Codex, Cline, Continue, Copilot, Q, Gemini, Cursor, and Windsurf configs remain native.
